This week, about 400 colleagues began their workday in a new place, as UOB marks an important milestone in our digital and innovation journey.

We became the first bank to move into the Punggol Digital District (PDD), Singapore’s first smart and sustainable business district!

Our S$500 million global technology and innovation centre at Tower 80 brings our technology and innovation teams together to build, test and deploy next‑generation banking capabilities across ASEAN. By October, over 3,000 technology, digital and innovation professionals will be based at this hub.

“By being the first bank to move into PDD, we are bringing our ideas and talents together at the heart of Singapore’s innovation ecosystem, advancing our digital‑first approach to re‑imagining banking systems and transforming the way we deliver value across our business. This strengthens our ability to build and scale future‑ready banking solutions to serve customers seamlessly across the ASEAN, while supporting Singapore’s Smart Nation ambitions,” said Lawrence Goh, our Head of Group Technology and Operations.

#RightByYou

【UOB Hong Kong appoints Cliff Law as Head of Corporate Banking】 

UOB Hong Kong today announced the appointment of Mr Cliff Law as Head of Corporate Banking, with immediate effect, succeeding Mr Anthony Tse.

Mr Law will lead the Bank’s corporate banking strategy and drive the continued growth of the franchise, while further strengthening UOB Hong Kong’s support for China–ASEAN business flows and enterprises with cross-border banking needs across the region. 

Cliff Law brings over two decades of banking experience across Hong Kong and Mainland China. Prior to joining UOB Hong Kong, he served as General Manager of Corporate Banking Division at a Hong Kong-headquartered bank. He also spent more than 20 years with a multinational bank, holding senior leadership roles across client coverage, loan syndications and financing solutions. His experience spans origination, structuring, underwriting and distribution of large scale China and cross-border transactions, supported by strong investor engagement and rigorous risk and compliance oversight.

“As we deepen our focus on capturing China-ASEAN business opportunities, Cliff’s extensive experience across China, including Hong Kong, and his corporate banking expertise will strengthen our ability to support clients’ cross-border ambitions and connect them to growth opportunities across ASEAN through UOB’s regional network,” commented Adaline Zheng, CEO of UOB Hong Kong.

Adaline also acknowledges Mr Anthony Tse for his contributions to UOB Hong Kong over a decade, playing an important role in strengthening the corporate banking franchise and advancing the Bank’s strategic priorities. On behalf of the Bank, she thanks Anthony for his dedication and commitment and wishes him every success in his future endeavours.

company_updates.reshared_post_description:
This week, I had the privilege of hosting and moderating an AI and Payments Innovation roundtable at UOB, bringing together leading voices across technology, academia, and public sector to exchange perspectives on the future of these two critical areas of growth in ASEAN.

The conversation was anchored with a key data point from our latest UOB Business Outlook Study report: AI adoption is accelerating, with 46% of businesses in ASEAN already using GenAI.

Several themes stood out during the discussion.
#ResponsibleAI remains an area where expectations are rising across the industry. As AI becomes more embedded in financial decisions, the bar for governance, accountability and transparency is moving higher—requiring continuous effort from all institutions.

A related reflection that surfaced during the discussion is that every technology brings with it two sides. AI, for instance, enables unprecedented levels of automation and efficiency, but also introduces new risks that must be actively managed. Similarly, real-time payments are highly desirable for their speed and convenience—but they also challenge traditional notions of recourse, dispute handling, and consumer protection. As we push the boundaries of innovation, being deliberate about these trade-offs becomes just as important as advancing the technology itself.

Another key theme was the interplay between technology and execution. While innovation in AI and payments continues to accelerate, the conversation increasingly points to execution as the differentiator—translating capability into reliable, scalable solutions that deliver consistent outcomes for customers.

Lastly, customer trust is being built through experience. Not through visibility of technology, but through end-to-end design—decisions that make sense, transactions that are seamless, and systems that work as expected.

Certainly, these conversations are converging—AI, payments and trust—and together, they will define the next phase of customer experience in banking across ASEAN.

Thank you for joining us—sincere appreciation for the candid and thoughtful exchange: Professor Louis Phee (Nanyang Technological University Singapore), Sau Sheong Chang (GovTech Singapore), Chia Hock Lai (Responsible Fintech Institute (RFI)), Varun Mahindru (Visa), Sutowo Wong (Temus), and Mike Sim (NGL Institute)

And also to colleagues Janet Young, Alvin Eng, Kah Kit Yip, and other team members at UOB for making this session possible.

I look forward to continuing this dialogue, as we collectively shape the future of banking in ASEAN!

#RightByYou #UOBInnovation #ResponsibleAI #FutureOfBanking

UOB MDS is a five-year programme designed to equip 90,000 students in Indonesia aged 6–17 across 500 schools in 38 provinces with coding and digital skills. Through this commitment, we aim to prepare the next generation to stay ahead in an increasingly digitalised economy.

During the visit, students took part in coding sessions, project presentations and financial literacy workshops, strengthening not only digital capabilities but also responsible financial habits from an early age.

Janet Young, UOB Group Head, Channels and Digitalisation, Strategic Communications and Brand; Luke Ariefiandi, Strategic Communications and Brand Head, UOB Indonesia; and Arman Wiratmoko, Chief Financial and Strategy Officer, Ruangguru, engaged directly with the students and educators.

At UOB, being progressive means investing in young minds today so they can shape tomorrow. Through initiatives like UOB MDS, we bring our purpose of building the future of ASEAN to life, because doing right by the children today builds a more resilient future for all.

ธนาคารยูโอบี ประเทศไทย จับมือกับ TikTok และ DHL เปิดตัวโครงการ Live-Commerce Accelerator ภายใต้แนวคิด “From Local to Viral” เพื่อช่วย SME ไทย สร้างยอดขาย ทำคอนเทนต์และ Live ได้อย่างมั่นใจ ใช้เครื่องมือดิจิทัลอย่างมืออาชีพ เติบโตได้อย่างยั่งยืน
.
ไฮไลต์สำคัญภายในงาน

- คุณยลสิริ หงษ์หิรัญเรือง Regional Marketing Manager, TikTok แนะนำ 5 กลยุทธ์สำคัญสู่ความสำเร็จ พร้อมอัปเดต Insight แพลตฟอร์ม TikTok

Innovation cycles are shortening as new technologies emerge. But the real challenge is not speed alone. It is ensuring that innovation delivers trusted, responsible and scalable outcomes that create meaningful impact.
 
Collaboration across academia, industry and the public sector is therefore essential. It brings together research depth, practical expertise, and the foundations needed for innovation to scale sustainably.
 
Last year, UOB and the Wee Foundation pledged a combined S$110 million to Nanyang Technological University Singapore. With government matching, the total impact of S$275 million will help level the playing field for less advantaged students, support community uplift programmes and catalyse innovation.
 
One programme to catalyse innovation is the NTU Venture Creation Programme, which will support up to 50 student teams each year with seed funding and mentorship to build commercial viability and scale for their ventures.
 
And this is the spirit behind the UOB Innovation Hub. Today, I am pleased to announce the launch of the new UOB Innovation Hub at the NTU campus – designed to bring academia and industry together to incubate viable deep‑tech ventures and nurture the next generation of entrepreneurs.
 
Alongside this, we are establishing the UOB Foundation to further strengthen our commitment to communities across ASEAN, with a focus on education, children and the arts.
 
Giving back to the community has always been part of UOB’s DNA. I hope that through these efforts, we can make a lasting impact and build a future where every young person has a fair chance to succeed.
 
#RightByYou #UOBFoundation

UOB Malaysia is honoured to be mandated as one of the Joint Lead Coordinators at the Signing Ceremony of the Joint Lead Coordinator (JLC) Appointment Agreement for FX transactions in relation to The East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) Project, organised by Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL).
 
The appointment marks the first coordinated foreign exchange risk management arrangement in Malaysia involving the Ministry of Finance and financial institutions. The ceremony formalised the collaboration between MRL and participating banks in support of the ECRL’s financial management framework. 
 
We are pleased to contribute our regional expertise in FX risk management in supporting the project’s objectives and the country’s long term development.
 
The ECRL is a strategically important national development that will reshape Malaysia’s logistics landscape by linking Port Klang with Kuantan Port and key economic zones along the east coast, enhancing connectivity and enabling new industrial and investment opportunities across multiple states,  enhancing Malaysia’s strategic position in ASEAN trade routes. 
 
With UOB’s strong regional network, we are well positioned to support businesses along these growing corridors as the ECRL advances and strengthens national connectivity and economic linkages.

UOB Group delivered a resilient full-year operating profit of S$7.7 billion for FY25, driven by strong fee momentum across wholesale and retail banking businesses.

While net interest income eased 3% yoy due to margin pressures from lower benchmark rates, net fee income grew 7% to a record high – a result of double-digit growth in wealth management and loan-related fees. Customer-related treasury income also reached a new record, backed by strong hedging and investment demand.

The Board declared a final dividend of 71 cents per ordinary share, bringing our total dividend to S$1.56 per share – representing a payout ratio of around 50%. In addition to the regular dividends, shareholders received a 50 cents special dividend that was paid over two tranches during 2025.

Looking ahead, we expect the region’s growth to continue to be powered by structural trends, including digitalisation, infrastructure investments and deepening regional integration. We are confident that our enlarged regional scale, stronger platforms and capabilities position us well to grow in tandem with the region.

UOB Malaysia has extended a US$150 million Sustainability-linked Loan (SLL) to RHB Banking Group, enhancing RHB’s financial flexibility to advance its sustainability commitments and support transition financing. The three-year facility will be used for general corporate and long-term funding needs, further strengthening RHB’s long-term funding profile while enabling the bank to embed sustainability considerations across its operations and to support customers, businesses and communities as they progress along their transition pathways.

“We are committed to advancing sustainable finance and working alongside like-minded partners who share this purpose. This collaboration with RHB reflects our broader commitment as a regional bank to steer capital towards responsible projects and enable credible transition pathways. As Malaysia moves toward a greener economy, we aim to contribute by helping raise sustainability standards across the industry and by supporting the country’s overall green agenda.” – Andy Cheah, Country Head of Wholesale Banking, UOB Malaysia

The three-year facility will be used for general corporate and long-term funding needs, further strengthening RHB’s long-term funding profile while enabling the bank to embed sustainability considerations across its operations and to support customers, businesses and communities as they progress along their transition pathways.

“We are committed to advancing sustainable finance and working alongside like-minded partners who share this purpose. This collaboration with RHB reflects our broader commitment as a regional bank to steer capital towards responsible projects and enable credible transition pathways. As Malaysia moves toward a greener economy, we aim to contribute by helping raise sustainability standards across the industry and by supporting the country’s overall green agenda.” – Andy Cheah, Country Head of Wholesale Banking, UOB Malaysia

Harapman Kasan, Wholesale Banking Director of UOB Indonesia, and Soichiro Nishimori, President Director of PT Alam Energy Renewables, signed a financing agreement worth IDR 128 billion through the U-Solar programme to strengthen collaboration in supporting the clean energy transition undertaken by our partners and customers.

Alam Energy Renewables by Shizen Energy Group is a solar panel development and leasing company and part of Shizen Group, a renewable energy specialist from Japan. This collaboration provides access to a strong, reliable, and globally recognised solar energy ecosystem.

Through this partnership, UOB Indonesia supports you, business leaders and entrepreneurs in advancing a more efficient and sustainable energy transition, while contributing to the growth of Indonesia’s solar ecosystem for the long-term benefit of all stakeholders.

This year’s design was modelled after the bonsai trees surrounding UOB Plaza and Plaza Atrium, embodying UOB’s belief that meaningful growth begins with intention and is strengthened through care, commitment and resilience.

We are delighted to partner with Singapore Manufacturing Federation (SMF) to launch the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone #JSSEZ playbook. This launch marks a key milestone in our partnership with SMF, enabled by close collaboration between UOB teams in Singapore and Malaysia to strengthen cross‑border supply chain connectivity under the JS‑SEZ.

The playbook is a practical guide for manufacturers to assess readiness, evaluate strategic plan and identify actionable steps to turn cross-border ambitions from vision to the factory floor. It provides new insights and practical recommendations on:

✅ Leveraging the SETUP framework as a strategic decision-making blueprint for entering the JS-SEZ
✅ Building an end-to-end financial architecture across corporate, workforce and individual needs
✅ Engineering future-ready and digitally connected factories that are scalable and resilient
 
“As supply chain and operational pressures intensify, diversifying operations is essential and the JS‑SEZ offers the ecosystem to strengthen efficiency and resilience, enabling regional growth and competitiveness,” said Jimmy Koh, our Group Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) Advisory’s Head of Network Partnerships and Strategic Marketing, at the Manufacturing Day Summit (#MDS2026) organised by SMF. Together with leaders from SMF, Enterprise Singapore, Malaysian Investment Development Authority and Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM), they discussed how the JS-SEZ can drive the next wave of regional manufacturing growth.

Start your JS‑SEZ journey today.

For many SMEs, one of the main barriers to AI adoption isn’t the technology – it is financing.

To help these firms with a clear financing pathway to kickstart their digital transformation, we recently signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the Association of Small & Medium Enterprises at the AIFA Finale Dinner 2026.

Through this partnership, ASME will mobilise up to S$10 million in existing grants and work with SMEs that come through SME@AITE to scope digital and AI projects, while we provide financial packages prior to grant disbursement.

During the signing witnessed by Minister of State for Trade and Industry Alvin Tan and ASME Vice President Hamidah Aidillah Mustafa (PBM), Paul Kan 简毅辉, our Country Head of SME Banking (Emerging Enterprise), Singapore, said, "The adoption of digital and AI tools are critical for SMEs to remain relevant and competitive in a rapidly evolving business landscape. We partner SMEs to build digital capabilities for the long term to strengthen their readiness to participate in broader business value chains. Our collaboration with ASME aims to accelerate SMEs’ digital transformation journey by closing the financing gap for them. This enables SMEs to minimise strain on their resources, so that they can focus on strategic growth and development."

Find out how your SME can benefit from this programme: https://go.uob.com/46gyQg7

"2026 Economic Outlook: Global Challenges, Asia’s Opportunities"

The economic landscape entering 2026 continues to be shaped by ongoing global trade tensions, evolving tariff policies, and the reconfiguration of  supply chains. At the recent UOB 2026 Economic Outlook in Hong Kong, Mr Heng Koon How, Head of Strategy, Global Economics and Markets Research at UOB, discussed the rise of de-dollarisation, China’s economic trajectory, and ASEAN’s increasingly important role in global growth.
 
According to UOB research, ASEAN continues to demonstrate resilience. Deeper regional integration and cross-border collaboration are reinforcing its position as a key driver of global economic growth. We expect real GDP in ASEAN to remain robust, with sustained momentum over the medium term.
 
Risks such as trade tensions and evolving policy environments remain part of the outlook. Even so, ASEAN’s adaptability is helping the region navigate these uncertainties and unlock new opportunities. Sectors such as electronics and artificial intelligence are reshaping global supply chains and creating potential for growth in Asia.
 
For more insights on the outlook for 2026, visit: https://go.uob.com/3ZfB8Z2

Yesterday, we welcomed Vietnam’s Standing Deputy Prime Minister, H.E. Mr Nguyen Hoa Binh to UOB during his official visit to Singapore. We discussed Vietnam’s strategic vision for its upcoming International Financial Centres (IFC) at Ho Chi Minh City and Da Nang, and how international financial institutions can participate in and support the development of Vietnam’s financial ecosystem.  

As the first Singapore bank to establish a presence in Vietnam more than 30 years ago, we have built deep local roots and strong capabilities. Today, as a locally incorporated bank, we provide a full suite of onshore solutions while leveraging our extensive ASEAN network for offshore connectivity – supporting Vietnamese enterprises expanding regionally, multinational companies investing in Vietnam, and international businesses entering the market.

“Vietnam is one of our key regional markets, and we have always believed in the country’s strong growth potential. Our long-term commitment is evident in our continued investments and growth, including the planned UOB Headquarters in Ho Chi Minh City’s International Financial Centre (IFC) area, which will house our enterprising and growing team of more than 1,500 staff. This strong foundation, combined with our ability to deliver both local execution and regional connectivity, is what truly differentiates our franchise.

“As Vietnam strengthens its investment and financial ecosystem through the development of IFCs, we see significant opportunities to facilitate capital flows, enhance cross-border trade, and contribute to sustainable economic growth. UOB is committed to contributing to Vietnam’s next stage of growth,” said Wee Ee Cheong, our Deputy Chairman and CEO.
